no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states implementation details: 'create a .link file containing the path', 'add .catch(logErrorAndExit)', 'check if (!traceLegend) and throws an error with message "Tracing is not started"', 'signature: load(backend: TraceLoaderBackend, traceFile?: string, unzipProgress?: UnzipProgress)'. solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_cli_program_catches_errors asserts the exact string 'cliProgram().catch(logErrorAndExit)' in the source. An alternative like wrapping in try/catch or using a different error handler name would fail. test_trace_loader_accepts_optional_tracefile asserts 'traceFile?:' which forces a specific parameter name. test_tracing_has_error_handling asserts 'if (!traceLegend)' (truncated but visible in test structure).

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Most fail-to-pass tests are pure text-matching: test_trace_loader_accepts_optional_tracefile, test_trace_loader_optional_unzipprogress, test_cli_program_catches_errors all just read source files and assert substrings. test_opentrace_creates_link_file_for_trace_files and test_loadtrace_reads_link_file run Node scripts but those scripts simulate the logic independently rather than importing the actual module code — they don't test the actual implementation.

test_not_tautological.reason: test_opentrace_creates_link_file_for_trace_files and test_loadtrace_reads_link_file run standalone Node scripts that re-implement the expected logic themselves rather than calling the actual project code. They will pass regardless of what the agent actually implements in the source files, as long as the compilation tests pass. The text-matching tests like test_trace_loader_accepts_optional_tracefile could be satisfied by just adding 'traceFile?:' anywhere in the file without actual functionality

behavior_in_tests.reason: All five behavioral requirements from instruction.md are covered: .link file creation, .link file reading, optional traceFile/unzipProgress parameters, CLI error handling, and traceLegend validation.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear sections (Gates/pass_to_pass, Fail-to-pass/pr_diff, Pass-to-pass/regression), descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining each test's purpose.
